speaker: MATT
raw_text: Moving on from that, as a reminder we are heading to San Diego Comic Con, like, in a day. As well as Ashly.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Yes.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: I love that you "lost" the printed-out, e-mail letters in our move.

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: Everyone used the same paper, stock, and font.

preceding_turns.speaker: ASHLEY
preceding_turns.text: They look really pristine.

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: All right. One of them was fake.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Thank you, Sam. And thank you, our friends at D&D Beyond, and I'm sorry.

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: You've got to laugh.

speaker: MATT
raw_text: Okay then. Last we left off, The Mighty Nein were making their way northward across the northern side of the Marrow Valley, toward the city of Shadycreek Run, to fulfill the second of two jobs they had accepted from The Gentleman while they were in Zadash. Along the journey, while they were leaving Hupperdook in the middle of the night, three of their members had been taken. It was discovered in the morning when they were nowhere to be found, that they had been captured by a group of slavers traveling north back to the city of Shadycreek Run itself. Upon investigating the details of this capture, you encountered an individual coming up the road with a similar aligning interest, Keg. In discussing the means of traveling to find these individuals responsible, and discovering that Keg had information on them, you decided to travel together making your way around the Crispvale Thicket. Encountering a number of indigenous, insect-like creatures known as ankhegs near the long-abandoned farmlands that were once built in the outskirts of the forest. Camping inside, you encountered a few members of a firbolg village that had long existed, hidden from the world within this thicket that had been assaulted by these same slavers, known as the Iron Shepherds. You encountered an elder protector of this tribe, along with a boy who had survived this encounter, and were sent off on your way. Traveling northward on horseback, you managed to catch up and actually make ground on the Iron Shepherds. Comparing your intel, and through a few surprises as to what they were carrying, a plan was hatched to set an ambush, to halt them in their tracks, and assault them. Upon the plan taking off, things were going in your favor, but through a series of bad lucks-- A bad lucks, the plural, it's an actual English phrase.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: You assume I ate breakfast. You assume I've eaten at all this week.

preceding_turns.speaker: ASHLEY
preceding_turns.text: Oh no!

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: I'm sure if Laura were here, she would say there are still many Nott the Best Detective Agency T-shirts in the store. Are there? I don't know.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: I don't know, I should probably--

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: Laura?

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: Okay, she's not here.

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: Travis, bring me pickles!

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: That's exactly what she'd be saying right now.

preceding_turns.speaker: ASHLEY
preceding_turns.text: Oh my god, that was a perfect Laura. I thought she was here!

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: Pretty spot on. I've known her a long time.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: All right then. That brings us into tonight's episode of Critical Role.

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: (scatting) [click, TV static] [groovy Critical Role theme]

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: --you!

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Welcome back. (laughter)

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: (frustrated sigh) So?

preceding_turns.speaker: ASHLEY
preceding_turns.text: This is too much emotional emotional whiplash. Can you stand for the full four hours?

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: I want to.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: No! Oh god!

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: Metal.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: I can see the wrinkles from where it was packaged.

speaker: MATT
raw_text: About two days' travel and it's maybe a two hour journey into the forest north of Shadycreek.

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: Beauregard, she would have told us. She wouldn't have been able to hide it.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: Where is his fucking stupid tapestry? Where is it?

preceding_turns.speaker: ASHLEY
preceding_turns.text: They are going to Shadycreek. They are probably going to take them to The Sour Nest, that's where they--

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: I go, and I look. Is his tapestry still on her horse?

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Yeah, the tapestry of Bahamut is still rolled up and strapped over the back of one of the horses.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: I storm over to it and I rip it off the horse and I say: This is ours now. He was our fucking friend, he wasn't your fucking friend. We're taking this back.

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: I pull Keg up. This place they are going, is it permanent? Or a temporary camp, or it's been there for years and this where they always are?

preceding_turns.speaker: ASHLEY
preceding_turns.text: It's where they always are.

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: Do you know how far off we are from it now?

preceding_turns.speaker: ASHLEY
preceding_turns.text: How far is Shadycreek?

speaker: MATT
raw_text: Close to 17 or so days since you left Zadash.

preceding_turns.speaker: ASHLEY
preceding_turns.text: Look, you guys don't have a lot of time for your friends. The ones that are captured.

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: We should bury him, and leave some sort of marker--

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: A note.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: I'm assuming Shadycreek doesn't have high level priests and the Empire and churches and stuff like that. Doesn't sound like the type of place that has--

preceding_turns.speaker: ASHLEY
preceding_turns.text: One of the last places you'd find something like that.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: Yeah.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: The only things that you would know of Shadycreek, from your experience, would be whatever miracle workers that would exist there are owned by the tribes, and weird tales of strange hermits that live in the forest beyond the city.

preceding_turns.speaker: ASHLEY
preceding_turns.text: Okay. But I haven't met anyone directly.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Not directly, no.

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: How many days' travel are we from the beginning of this journey?

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: From the beginning?

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: Yeah. From The Gentleman.

speaker: MATT
raw_text: It's early morning.

preceding_turns.speaker: SUMALEE
preceding_turns.text: Why would you not?

preceding_turns.speaker: ASHLEY
preceding_turns.text: You saw the fight, right, lady?

preceding_turns.speaker: SUMALEE
preceding_turns.text: Yes. I can help.

preceding_turns.speaker: ASHLEY
preceding_turns.text: It's up to you guys. It's your funeral.

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: Your son has been captured?

preceding_turns.speaker: SUMALEE
preceding_turns.text: And my partner. Jumnda, the lady you ran into, my elder, she told me not to come, not to leave. "Accept nature's fate," she said. But I cannot. I have been kind and peaceful and to myself many years. No more. You need me and I cannot do this alone.

preceding_turns.speaker: ASHLEY
preceding_turns.text: Need you?

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: She's kind of right.

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: Hey, over there is a tree stump on the side of the road. That's the bald one. What do you do?

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: Show us.

preceding_turns.speaker: SUMALEE
preceding_turns.text: I will tell you because I cannot do it all the time, but when I am ready, I will summon lightning from the sky and fry him, destroy him! And I can do it again and again and again!

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: That sounds good.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: That sounds great.

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: We'd appreciate that.

preceding_turns.speaker: ASHLEY
preceding_turns.text: So moving?

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: Holy shit.

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: What time of the day is it?

speaker: MATT
raw_text: The fight was right at dawn.

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: Your son has been captured?

preceding_turns.speaker: SUMALEE
preceding_turns.text: And my partner. Jumnda, the lady you ran into, my elder, she told me not to come, not to leave. "Accept nature's fate," she said. But I cannot. I have been kind and peaceful and to myself many years. No more. You need me and I cannot do this alone.

preceding_turns.speaker: ASHLEY
preceding_turns.text: Need you?

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: She's kind of right.

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: Hey, over there is a tree stump on the side of the road. That's the bald one. What do you do?

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: Show us.

preceding_turns.speaker: SUMALEE
preceding_turns.text: I will tell you because I cannot do it all the time, but when I am ready, I will summon lightning from the sky and fry him, destroy him! And I can do it again and again and again!

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: That sounds good.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: That sounds great.

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: We'd appreciate that.

preceding_turns.speaker: ASHLEY
preceding_turns.text: So moving?

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: Holy shit.

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: What time of the day is it?

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: It's early morning.

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: Metal o'clock.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Apparently!

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: The fight was in the early morning.

speaker: MATT
raw_text: It's maybe an hour after sunrise at this point, and the sky is still mostly clouded and becoming lighter. The flurry is coming closer, there is a gentle snowfall coming down at this early hour.

preceding_turns.speaker: SUMALEE
preceding_turns.text: And my partner. Jumnda, the lady you ran into, my elder, she told me not to come, not to leave. "Accept nature's fate," she said. But I cannot. I have been kind and peaceful and to myself many years. No more. You need me and I cannot do this alone.

preceding_turns.speaker: ASHLEY
preceding_turns.text: Need you?

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: She's kind of right.

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: Hey, over there is a tree stump on the side of the road. That's the bald one. What do you do?

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: Show us.

preceding_turns.speaker: SUMALEE
preceding_turns.text: I will tell you because I cannot do it all the time, but when I am ready, I will summon lightning from the sky and fry him, destroy him! And I can do it again and again and again!

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: That sounds good.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: That sounds great.

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: We'd appreciate that.

preceding_turns.speaker: ASHLEY
preceding_turns.text: So moving?

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: Holy shit.

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: What time of the day is it?

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: It's early morning.

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: Metal o'clock.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Apparently!

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: The fight was in the early morning.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: The fight was right at dawn.

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: Right, okay.

speaker: MATT
raw_text: As you guys gather up onto your horses, Keg takes the lead and you all begin your trek northward once more. Both memory of Mollymauk behind you on one shoulder, and the image of the face of your vengeance before you. You continue on your journey for the next hour or so before you catch a glance, Keg, at the road above. You see three shapes in the road.

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: That is very lovely.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: Yeah. I'm going to go start mounting up the horses.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: You gather the last of your things, retrieve the horses, and head out.

preceding_turns.speaker: ASHLEY
preceding_turns.text: I'll lead the horses, since I know where I'm going.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Okay.

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: Yeah, we have three horses-- oh wait, you had one too. Wait, did you? No, you were on foot. We had three left.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Yeah, you had three left after the previous battle.

preceding_turns.speaker: ASHLEY
preceding_turns.text: I have to ride on one by myself.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Correct. You guys can double up.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: Yeah, it was you two doubled up and I had one.

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: You are probably going to have to share-- Do you have a horse?

preceding_turns.speaker: SUMALEE
preceding_turns.text: I can be a horse.

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: Oh, you are a horse! Of course.

preceding_turns.speaker: SUMALEE
preceding_turns.text: I can turn into a horse once more. But if I do, I think that is the last time I can do it today.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Until you take a rest.

preceding_turns.speaker: SUMALEE
preceding_turns.text: Until my next rest.

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: Horse nap.

speaker: MATT
raw_text: Keg leading on to the north, you diverge from the Glory Run Road, heading into the high grasses of the North Marrow Valley. You continue on for the better part of the day, taking breaks as necessary, and for you to recover your forms. You continue pushing on-- not at a breakneck pace for fear of catching up to your quarry, but enough where you know at least you are keeping pace. Eventually the light of the sky begins to grow dim as the evening calls to you, at which point the flurry has come. The snow begins to get heavier, the darkness is darker than normal at dusk and the air gets bitter cold. What do you wish to do to set camp?

preceding_turns.speaker: ASHLEY
preceding_turns.text: Slip in.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: You do know that just beyond the Breach, there is a gate that is guarded by one of the families of the tribes. There is a toll that's taken as you pass through.

preceding_turns.speaker: ASHLEY
preceding_turns.text: Okay. That would probably be a good idea.

preceding_turns.speaker: SUMALEE
preceding_turns.text: I can do that.

preceding_turns.speaker: ASHLEY
preceding_turns.text: Thanks.

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: For you, we'll find a fake mustache or something?

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: Are you able to do just one person?

preceding_turns.speaker: SUMALEE
preceding_turns.text: No, I think I'm able to do--

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: Could you do the whole group?

preceding_turns.speaker: SUMALEE
preceding_turns.text: Yes, I can, right?

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: Right, God? (laughter)

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: Is it Pass Without a Trace you're looking at?

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: That can help them hide if they want.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: Yes, so as long as we stay within 30 feet of you, we get plus ten to our stealth bonus, which would be very helpful.

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: We don't turn invisible.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: Correct.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: If anybody wants to hide, it does help out.

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: You guys could hide under the horses or something.

preceding_turns.speaker: ASHLEY
preceding_turns.text: Okay.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: That's badass. I like that.

speaker: MATT
raw_text: As you guys continue your trek northward for the next day, you slowly push forward and watch as the rolling hills of this northern valley begin to even out. The high grasses growing dense and dusted with snow in the wake of this snowstorm that has passed through the night. It's now this beautiful, ice topped wonderland before you. As the storm is past, breaks in the clouds begin to slowly emerge and show you glimmers of blue in the sky. With sunlight comes a welcome comparative warmth, and this dusted field of diamonds begins to slowly thaw over the midday's journey. The looming peaks of the Dunrock mountain range approach closer, engulfing your horizon almost like a toothy jaw of some ancient titan that surrounds your peripheral. You can make out, as you get closer, the craggy break in the mountains ahead, familiar to you. It's like a spiral fracture of a bone, where suddenly what was once a solid mountain range seems to have torn and sundered into two separate ravines that seem to share a similar shape. This you recognize as the Quannah Breach. You guys begin to approach, by mid to late afternoon, the front of the breach, where you can see six crownsguard in a small outpost built at the base of the Quannah Breach. These, as you know, are usually folks that essentially warn people from traveling into the Breach, and if they do, let them know that any return is going to be probably highly searched and highly inquired. They enforce it, unless you slip them some coin. Then it's not an issue on the way back. They're more there to make money.

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: Sam, you're bonier than Gollum.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Keg, in full metal armor, not so much.

preceding_turns.speaker: ASHLEY
preceding_turns.text: I add my modifier though, yes?

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: You do add your modifier.

preceding_turns.speaker: ASHLEY
preceding_turns.text: Then it's actually 15.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Yeah, you're fine. You shrug it off without issue.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Caleb, your warm coat keeps you warm.

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: Hearty German living.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Exactly. You're used to these Zemnian nights.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: That's enough to withstand it, not a problem.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: While the forest definitely keeps insulation to the storm, your body is well suited for extreme weather as well. Actually-- tricksy winter clothes, but you got it.

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: No, you can--

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: No, you got them for a reason. That's what they're there for. Because of the body heat and everything, I'll allow it.

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: You're like the little kid in A Christmas Story, "I can't put my arms down!"

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: So cute.

speaker: MATT
raw_text: As you guys continue your trek northward for the next day, you slowly push forward and watch as the rolling hills of this northern valley begin to even out. The high grasses growing dense and dusted with snow in the wake of this snowstorm that has passed through the night. It's now this beautiful, ice topped wonderland before you. preceding_turns.speaker: ASHLEY

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: You do add your modifier.

preceding_turns.speaker: ASHLEY
preceding_turns.text: Then it's actually 15.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Caleb, your warm coat keeps you warm.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: That's enough to withstand it, not a problem.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: No, you can--

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: No, you got them for a reason. That's what they're there for. Because of the body heat and everything, I'll allow it.

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: You're like the little kid in A Christmas Story, "I can't put my arms down!"

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: So cute.

speaker: MATT
raw_text: This is the first town any of you have been in that has no watch. No law keepers. The road doesn't go far north from where you enter as most of the town is this long stretched bar, so within 15 minutes you've hit the plaza. The central plaza to the town that you know as the Clover Plaza. You see log benches dotting the open area, while a four story tower of motley colored stones stands vigil over the area. Two balconies and a top, housing crossbow and longbow wielding roughnecks. You know these to be Taskers. You're heading towards The Landlocked Lady? Easy to see. On the eastern side of this plaza, there is a very beautiful, two story complex, that comparably seems ostentatious against the buildings immediately to the right and left of it which look to be in serious disrepair and rather rundown and colorless. Just bland woods, gray and brown. Here you can see the second story above the main archway, a curved top arch, there is a false bow of a ship that is emerging outward. This beautifully carved mermaid in the front that is clutching the piercing front of the ship. There it rests with a sign dangling from it, saying The Landlocked Lady.

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: (spits)

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: Ugh, gross.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: An emaciated dog growls at you from a nearby doorstep and then darts off down the road behind where you passed. A wagon appears stuck in the mud as you pass it, rough-looking folk tugging at it and stopping to stare you down as you slowly move past. They continue their work. You nearly step over someone with your horse who appears to be passed out, face down in a patch of dirty snow. A bit of crimson rests at their side. They're not moving. As you circle around them you watch as two rough-looking fellows in hide clad armor come and inspect him. As you glance over your shoulder, you watch as they rummage through his pockets, lift up his not moving body and dump him in one of the nearby alleys and keep on walking. You see no Crownsguard.

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: Oh man.

speaker: MATT
raw_text: The Sour Nest is northeast of where you are in the city. It's beyond this city, into the actual Savalierwood. About maybe two hours' travel.

preceding_turns.speaker: ASHLEY
preceding_turns.text: I think it's probably best for me not to be.

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: Let's go in the brothel. We're here, right? We can stay here, or we go in here? Do I have to fuck somebody in there?

preceding_turns.speaker: ASHLEY
preceding_turns.text: Why are you worried about this? They would be worried about having to fuck you, my man.

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: I'm a lady.

preceding_turns.speaker: ASHLEY
preceding_turns.text: My lady. I call everyone my man.

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: But yes, you're right. You're right, that's a fair point.

preceding_turns.speaker: ASHLEY
preceding_turns.text: If any of the Marduns are there, we might be able to-- to be honest guys, there's not a lot of options, but we might be able to get some help from them. I don't know for sure. In any case, it's a place to regroup. This brothel is not in the business of spilling everybody's secrets out.

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: Let's go in.

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: Ja, one second, though. Which way from here are these Iron Chefs located?

preceding_turns.speaker: ASHLEY
preceding_turns.text: They're north of this, yes, Matt? The Sour Nest is?

speaker: MATT
raw_text: "Her. That is a preference. Very well." Goes and writes something down. "Can be done. For a night's companionship, do you wish just for an evening's stay, or for the night over?"

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: Yeah, let's formulate a plan in the morning. I can't think right now. I'm a little out of it.

preceding_turns.speaker: ASHLEY
preceding_turns.text: All right. Champ, she sleeps alone. No funny business, all right?

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: "Of course not. I have a business to run."

preceding_turns.speaker: SUMALEE
preceding_turns.text: Thank you.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: I'll take a room for myself, please.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: "All right. For a singular room rate for the night, that will be one gold, five silver."

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: I arrange for companionship and drinks.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: "Any preference of companion? We have a spectrum to choose from."

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: It feels weird going and picking someone out. That feels dirty.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: "Would you prefer it a surprise?"

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: Yeah. Just make her hot, please.

speaker: MATT
raw_text: It's nestled into the forest even further north of where the city stops in the forest. Continuing on you begin to enter the canopy of the south Savalierwood. The shade completely consumes the city at this point, even though it's mid-morning and the clouds are broken up so that occasional bits of warmth and sunlight would occasionally pass through the city, now it seems like it's almost dusk immediately. Occasionally you see a leaf tumble past, and then another. It's almost beautiful, the color, but it does have almost a decaying sense to its meeting between grey and purple. It's beautiful in light, and then when it hits the ground it almost looks like it's bereft of life. You don't understand the nature of this enchantment, whatever this curse may be that you've heard of.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Not bad. That works. You've been at this for a while. The Savalierwood has been cursed for many centuries. The once-green forest now stands a greyish purple ashen color that it now holds and the superstition and mythology around it has allowed the city of Shadycreek Run to prevent Empire encroachment. They built the city into the space, and every time the Empire has attempted to move forward they've pulled back and used it as a defensive position. Essentially, over a number of conflicts, the Empire just gave up, and let the region exist on its own. It is dangerous, the wood. The deeper in, the more dangerous it gets.

preceding_turns.speaker: ASHLEY
preceding_turns.text: And The Sour Nest is nestled into it.

speaker: MATT
raw_text: It lasts for ten minutes.

preceding_turns.speaker: SUMALEE
preceding_turns.text: It says it's been this way a long time.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: Wow. I mean, how long is long for a bird though?

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: They only live for three weeks, right? That's probably a bad question.

preceding_turns.speaker: SUMALEE
preceding_turns.text: This bird lives a long time, I can tell you. This type of bird is not a short-lived bird.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: Dagon can live to be 30. She's going to outlive us all.

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: Would you like to ask it what is dangerous in the wood?

preceding_turns.speaker: SUMALEE
preceding_turns.text: Yes. What is most dangerous here?

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: "Be wary of spirits."

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: Ooh, spirits.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: "Be wary of bone-spurred beasts."

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: Bone-spurred beasts. Okay.

preceding_turns.speaker: ASHLEY
preceding_turns.text: Doesn't sound great.

preceding_turns.speaker: SUMALEE
preceding_turns.text: What other questions? Are you still willing to answer some more?

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: How long does the spell last?

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: Three days.

preceding_turns.speaker: ASHLEY
preceding_turns.text: The bird's a party member now.

speaker: MATT
raw_text: You see all the other guards each place one of their weapons pointed directly at each of you, and the quiet comes over all of you, as this moment, this standoff continues for a minute, two minutes. As the first guard that you spoke to enters and closes the door behind.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: Can I look around, do I see anybody who's hidden, watching from windows up above, anything that looks like we're being watched?

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Make a perception check.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: Not by guards, necessarily. 21.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: You glance up, and on the second floor there is two sets of double windows. They're paned, one of them looks to be partially opened, and you can see on the inside there are curtains. One, the curtains are closed, the other is drawn to a side, and you see a figure looking out below. As soon as you look up and see it, the curtain closes.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: Did I catch a glimpse of what the figure looks like?

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: The figure looked to be human, though the skin was dark. That's as best as you can get before they vanished.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: Okay.

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: I'm just smiling at the guards.

speaker: MATT
raw_text: A moment later the guard emerges from the door. "All right, stand down."

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: Did I catch a glimpse of what the figure looks like?

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: The figure looked to be human, though the skin was dark. That's as best as you can get before they vanished.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: Okay.

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: I'm just smiling at the guards.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: You see all the other guards each place one of their weapons pointed directly at each of you, and the quiet comes over all of you, as this moment, this standoff continues for a minute, two minutes. As the first guard that you spoke to enters and closes the door behind.

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: (exhales)

preceding_turns.speaker: ASHLEY
preceding_turns.text: So do you like being guards?

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: "Shh!" One of them tightens the grip.

preceding_turns.speaker: ASHLEY
preceding_turns.text: Got it.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: This large, heavy crossbow already notched backward and the tension on the wire itself is-- this guy is looking like he is ready to fire.

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: We are all going to laugh about this later, I promise. (nervous laugh)

speaker: MATT
raw_text: "Open the gate." The guard who was eating earlier, puts the shortbow over the shoulder, still looking wary, and they're not moving from where they're standing. They're all keeping a very close eye on all five of you. Opens the gate (clicking) (creaking). The metallic gate swings open, and you're all brought into the courtyard. As you're begin to follow the first guard you spoke with, they all slowly coalesce behind you, still keeping their weapons at the side, but ready if need be. As you're led to the interior chamber of this estate, the Estate Sybaritic. As soon as you enter the main foyer, the earthy smell of the forest gives way to the lingering scent of a recent meal. As if the cooked meats and starches that were eaten maybe an hour or two beforehand still permeate the air of this entryway. You see before you a staircase that continues upward, red carpeted, before switching back to a second floor. In that center landing you can see a very open window that looks down to possibly a backyard of some kind, but currently the view is of the darkened leaves and bits of sunlight that break through. It seems like something that was built for a beautiful view, not understanding until it was completed that it didn't really catch much of a view amongst this forest, like the original builder had brought sensibilities from outside to bring them in, and it was not compatible with this landscape. There, you see to the left and right of you art pieces that depict people. You can see individuals of different backgrounds, and you see one that appears to be of pure elven blood with brown hair, difficult to make out the gender, but beautiful and standing very robust with a chin out and a wily grin. Oils, very well done. To the left of you, you can see the other portrait appears to be a woman with a very well made dress with these large, almost puffs at the shoulders. It seems almost trying too hard to be aristocratic. It's garish, but here you are. A moment later the door closes behind you, and you're still being flanked by this bevy of guards. You hear the heavy footsteps coming down the staircase. Turning the corner, you see a figure now, framed in what little of the light is coming through that window behind them. (footfalls) Stepping down you see a woman, dark-gray skin and ankle-length, black hair that is smooth and straight that falls behind her. You see bony crests of horns that curve up into these two large points behind the head and bright yellow eyes that are pupil-less. A grin that shows two fanged canines. As she descends you can see her attire is very formal, colonial style coat with epaulets, but it seems to be tailored to not hide her figure. She already exudes an air of powerful aggression and presence. With each step that she takes down there, you can sense her immediately reading and seeing in and through each of you. Quietly she takes the last step to the landing here in the foyer. (German accent) "You bring tidings from The Gentleman, you say."

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: Okay.

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: I'm just smiling at the guards.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: You see all the other guards each place one of their weapons pointed directly at each of you, and the quiet comes over all of you, as this moment, this standoff continues for a minute, two minutes. As the first guard that you spoke to enters and closes the door behind.

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: (exhales)

preceding_turns.speaker: ASHLEY
preceding_turns.text: So do you like being guards?

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: "Shh!" One of them tightens the grip.

preceding_turns.speaker: ASHLEY
preceding_turns.text: Got it.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: This large, heavy crossbow already notched backward and the tension on the wire itself is-- this guy is looking like he is ready to fire.

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: We are all going to laugh about this later, I promise. (nervous laugh)

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: A moment later the guard emerges from the door. "All right, stand down."

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: Oh.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: They all pull their weapons away, looking disappointed.

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: We are good?

speaker: MATT
raw_text: "Open the gate." The guard who was eating earlier, puts the shortbow over the shoulder, still looking wary, and they're not moving from where they're standing. They're all keeping a very close eye on all five of you. Opens the gate (clicking) (creaking). The metallic gate swings open, and you're all brought into the courtyard. You see bony crests of horns that curve up into these two large points behind the head and bright yellow eyes that are pupil-less. A grin that shows two fanged canines. Quietly she takes the last step to the landing here in the foyer. (German accent) "You bring tidings from The Gentleman, you say."

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: Okay.

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: I'm just smiling at the guards.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: You see all the other guards each place one of their weapons pointed directly at each of you, and the quiet comes over all of you, as this moment, this standoff continues for a minute, two minutes. As the first guard that you spoke to enters and closes the door behind.

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: (exhales)

preceding_turns.speaker: ASHLEY
preceding_turns.text: So do you like being guards?

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: "Shh!" One of them tightens the grip.

preceding_turns.speaker: ASHLEY
preceding_turns.text: Got it.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: This large, heavy crossbow already notched backward and the tension on the wire itself is-- this guy is looking like he is ready to fire.

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: We are all going to laugh about this later, I promise. (nervous laugh)

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: A moment later the guard emerges from the door. "All right, stand down."

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: Oh.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: They all pull their weapons away, looking disappointed.

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: We are good?

speaker: MATT
raw_text: "Thorough, I appreciate that. Well so, you are the help that he sent. Timely, three days earlier would have been better, but here we are. I shall not fret on it. Thank you for coming. Be at ease, go watch the back walls, make sure nobody tries to sneak in while we're talking." The guards all scatter, except for two who stay posted at each side of the door behind you. "How much have you been told?"

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: That is correct, ja.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: "Well you have invoked the name of my comrade. You have my attention, do not squander it. Why are you here?"

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: Well, we will be plain. He has sent us. He has told us that you are in need of assistance, and he has deemed us worthy to provide it.

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: Are you Ophelia?

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: "I am."

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: Oh, just making sure.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: Yeah, that's good.

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: It could be someone else.

speaker: MATT
raw_text: You watch as her eyes looks you from head to toe and her tongue runs across her lip a bit. "(sighs) I've had an open line of shipments to Zadash, through our mutual friend for some time, made a beneficial arrangement for many years."

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: That is correct, ja.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: "Well you have invoked the name of my comrade. You have my attention, do not squander it. Why are you here?"

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: Well, we will be plain. He has sent us. He has told us that you are in need of assistance, and he has deemed us worthy to provide it.

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: Are you Ophelia?

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: "I am."

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: Oh, just making sure.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: Yeah, that's good.

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: It could be someone else.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: "Thorough, I appreciate that. Well so, you are the help that he sent. Timely, three days earlier would have been better, but here we are. I shall not fret on it. Thank you for coming. Be at ease, go watch the back walls, make sure nobody tries to sneak in while we're talking." The guards all scatter, except for two who stay posted at each side of the door behind you. "How much have you been told?"

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: (small laugh) Not very much.

speaker: MATT
raw_text: "However, my most trusted team of smugglers that handle that route and knew it well were slain a little over a month ago."

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: (small laugh) Not very much.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: You watch as her eyes looks you from head to toe and her tongue runs across her lip a bit. "(sighs) I've had an open line of shipments to Zadash, through our mutual friend for some time, made a beneficial arrangement for many years."

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: Can I do an insight check? Did it look like she cast a spell just then?

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Sure, make an insight check.

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: Oh, whispers.

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: Whispers.

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: Fireball. (laughter)

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: Guys--

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: D&D Beyond.

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: Am I right?

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: Is a really good app.

preceding_turns.speaker: ASHLEY
preceding_turns.text: (flatly) It's so great.

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: It's like-- I can be on the shitter, if I want to check--

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: You can customize that.

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: Caleb's spells.

preceding_turns.speaker: ASHLEY
preceding_turns.text: I can?!

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Oh god.

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: You can with their new upgrade.

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: I can be mid dook.

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: You can be mid dook?

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: Mid dook and check my spells.

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: You're mid dook right now.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Anyway, bringing it back in.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: Shipments to Zadash.

speaker: MATT
raw_text: From where you are now, it's probably a two hour walk. It's the far east side. It's technically past the-- let's see, I have all the locations here. Pardon me as I pull out my notes.

preceding_turns.speaker: ASHLEY
preceding_turns.text: Can I tell where The Sour Nest would be from here?

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Make a survival check.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: With how far you've traveled north, it would be straight due east from here.

preceding_turns.speaker: ASHLEY
preceding_turns.text: Okay. Sour Nest is east of here.

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: You know where you are?

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: Should we go run recon? Take a quick scouting pass and see what we can see?

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: Yeah. Let's put a little distance between ourselves and this place and this gentleman and I will send Frumpkin out to have a look.

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: Okay.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: You make your way through the Savalierwood.

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: Is it a long walk? Or a long travel?

speaker: MATT
raw_text: Caleb's feeling a bit clumsy, but the rest of you, keeping up the slack, have managed to stop him at times and make him aware of his terrible attempts to stay out of sight and stay quiet. Two hours of travel eastward--

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: Stop it. I am nauseous. Let's go kill a bunch of people.

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: Give him a goodberry.

preceding_turns.speaker: SUMALEE
preceding_turns.text: I have a goodberry.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: Oh, yes!

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: That is sour.

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: It's a Purin tablet.

preceding_turns.speaker: SUMALEE
preceding_turns.text: One moment, it will take effect soon.

preceding_turns.speaker: ASHLEY
preceding_turns.text: Oh. End that way.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: Keep it down.

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: Let's go.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: It does warm your belly, and that warmth spreads through your torso and it's-- whenever Jester has come and pulled you from the edge of death with a spell, it's a fragment of that warmth from the inside of your torso.

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: 'Member?

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: 'Member berries. 'Member Jester?

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: 'Member clerics?

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: Oh yeah, I 'member.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Following Keg through the wood, I would like you all to make stealth checks, please.

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: Stealthy!

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: You are no longer under the Pass without a Trace spell.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: You get all of your spells back after sleeping as well.

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: Four!

speaker: MATT
raw_text: About an hour into it, a sound catches your ear, Keg. It catches yours as well. (growling) You hear the faint breaking of tinder and dry branch.

preceding_turns.speaker: SUMALEE
preceding_turns.text: Yes, I have two times now, right? Because I rested?

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Correct. Two times every short rest.

preceding_turns.speaker: SUMALEE
preceding_turns.text: I can do twice.

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: If you were to become, say, a raccoon right now and go running around, that is one time that you cannot become a crocodile to eat somebody's face off.

preceding_turns.speaker: SUMALEE
preceding_turns.text: Yes, and I would like to eat somebody's face off.

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: Okay. I think, let us reserve that for now because Frumpkin can do the job. Let's get closer to this place and it would only take five minutes. He will go.

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: In owl form?

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: Yeah.

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: Okay. He'll only be able to see from the sky. We should still do that, though, first.

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: I mean, I could turn him back into a cat, but then he's slower, he can't get over the walls maybe.

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: Go for it.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: Just send the bird.

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: How far have we traveled at this point?

speaker: MATT
raw_text: It's pretty dense. The reason it took two hours to get there is because you're going through the forest. Otherwise on foot, in any sort of open scenario, it would take you half the time.

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: You guys, Taliesin's a bear. (laughter)

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: You do manage to just skirt along the side of the bear without any notice from their side. Continuing onward. The secondary hour goes without issue, but you do begin to come upon the outskirts of your recollection of where The Sour Nest resides. You're still a good 100 or so feet away, using the trees at your disposal and you can see now the wall that matches the perimeter. It's a thin, five foot wide wall with a very flimsy rail across the top, but it is a walkway for watch guards to keep an eye out. As you glance at this distance, you can see one figure that's walking the perimeter of the top of the wall, with a heavy crossbow at the ready. They stop and look out.

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: How dense is the forest around this place?

speaker: MATT
raw_text: After a minute or so, the guard just keeps looking out. Jumpy. From Frumpkin's view, down inside, you can see the exterior of this. There is a large collection of brown wood kegs and a couple of small crates that contain materials that you're unaware of. You can see there are two entrances, one at the front and one at the back of the building. There are not windows, but small holes put into the wood and from the inside-- Actually, roll a perception check for Frumpkin, thank you. With advantage, due to the form, because it is with sight.

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: This is where we send an owl.

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: Sure.

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: I'm going to send him and it's going to take a moment. I want him to go around the entire place and keep an eye on it and watch it. Maybe 20 or 30 minutes. Are we good with that?

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: In his talons, he could bring a little mouse and drop it in there. I'm just saying.

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: How will we-- You and I could talk to the mouse.

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: We could, but the mouse couldn't talk back.

preceding_turns.speaker: ASHLEY
preceding_turns.text: Wait, wait, wait, what's our plan here?

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: Well, for now this is easy, we're going to send my owl now. If anything goes wrong, will you please tap three times on my left knee? I go blind and send Frumpkin up.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Okay, so, Frumpkin makes it up. Tree, waits a moment, tree. The one guard you see doing the perimeter notices.

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: I tell him telepathically to just chill and sit there for a while.

speaker: MATT
raw_text: For you, it's half your druid level, rounded down, so per transformation, it's about two hours.

preceding_turns.speaker: ASHLEY
preceding_turns.text: Do I have some sense of that?

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: You didn't work close enough with them to know of any secret passage. I mean, it makes sense. There might be one, but nothing that you've come across. If there is one, it's a well-guarded secret. You have no idea.

preceding_turns.speaker: ASHLEY
preceding_turns.text: Has anything changed since last I was here, that I can tell?

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Not really. They're not into renovations.

preceding_turns.speaker: ASHLEY
preceding_turns.text: Sure.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: If it works, stick with it.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: Do the guards sleep here on the estate, or do they go home at night?

preceding_turns.speaker: ASHLEY
preceding_turns.text: I'd assume they'd stay on the estate.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: They sleep on the estate. They're paid essentially as live-in employ guards.

preceding_turns.speaker: ASHLEY
preceding_turns.text: I don't know how we do this.

preceding_turns.speaker: SUMALEE
preceding_turns.text: I just want to see my son. I want to go inside.

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: You want to go in and scout around?

preceding_turns.speaker: ASHLEY
preceding_turns.text: How long does your magic last?

preceding_turns.speaker: SUMALEE
preceding_turns.text: Good question.

speaker: MATT
raw_text: You can! And in a few days, you'll probably make your way through.

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: Maybe there's a little crack or something.

preceding_turns.speaker: SUMALEE
preceding_turns.text: That's what I think is my way downstairs, so I go toward the trapdoor, see if I can't get in.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Make a strength check in your mouse form.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: Try and squeeze under? This is when a natural 20 is-- aw.

preceding_turns.speaker: SUMALEE
preceding_turns.text: Nine, that's a nine.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Yeah, no. I'll put that at a minus four. Your mouse isn't strong enough to budge the door that is currently--

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: There might be a hole in the floor or something else.

preceding_turns.speaker: SUMALEE
preceding_turns.text: I feel like that's the key, but I want to keep looking to see how many Iron Shepherds there may be in any other location that I haven't explored.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Okay, make a perception check.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: As you're pushing against the door, you do hear voices muffled from below. Then you hear what sounds like a laugh, a deep billowing laugh.

preceding_turns.speaker: SUMALEE
preceding_turns.text: Can I gnaw my way through the door?

speaker: MATT
raw_text: Okay, you scurry around the hallway. There is nobody in there, so there is no one you are trying to hide from. You make it back to the main entryway, head to the staircase, and it takes you awhile to get up there, because the steps are about that high each. It's a little jump, and then you miss a few and have to stop and skitter back up. It takes you awhile, the better part of about 20 to 25 minutes. You eventually make your way to the second floor. Up there, you can see another hallway and as opposed to the bottom floor, which had an L-shaped hallway that went around, this one has a perimeter hall. There are those porthole windows that are carved into the stone, two on each side. As you come up to the top, the hall goes around and there's a central chamber on the inside, or chambers if they're divided. You can see immediately on the second floor, a door directly available to the one that's just north of you. You don't see any other door on this particular corridor.

preceding_turns.speaker: SUMALEE
preceding_turns.text: I hear a laugh. Oh, have I heard this laugh before?

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: You have.

preceding_turns.speaker: SUMALEE
preceding_turns.text: Shit. This is enough for me, I know they're down there. The leader of the Iron Shepherds is down there beneath that trapdoor. That's what I believe. I don't know what to do now.

preceding_turns.speaker: ASHLEY
preceding_turns.text: Can you guys talk to her?

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: We can send a Message.

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: After a while, yeah.

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: I'll send a Message saying  Are you still alive?

preceding_turns.speaker: SUMALEE
preceding_turns.text: (squeaks)

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: She's still alive.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Sumalee, what are you going to do?

preceding_turns.speaker: SUMALEE
preceding_turns.text: I am going to stick to my mission to scout. I am going to pull everything I have to go away from the trap door, knowing where it is, and I am going to scurry up and I want to explore that spiral staircase.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: You're heading back into the cross-hallway. Are you following down the hall or are you heading back through the mess room?

preceding_turns.speaker: SUMALEE
preceding_turns.text: I'll go the hallway this time.

speaker: MATT
raw_text: Until nightfall? I'd say with the two-hours travel from the morning, you'd have probably another six seven hours.

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: One of these in the mess hall?

preceding_turns.speaker: SUMALEE
preceding_turns.text: No, just the leader that was eating something that didn't look like an animal. It looked small.

preceding_turns.speaker: ASHLEY
preceding_turns.text: Jesus Christ.

preceding_turns.speaker: SUMALEE
preceding_turns.text: Like my son's size.

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: How many people? You said two?

preceding_turns.speaker: SUMALEE
preceding_turns.text: There were six or seven beds, and I heard voices under the trap door. I know they're there. My son is not dead. He is there underneath the trap door.

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: I pull some paper out and say, "Can you draw these rooms?"

preceding_turns.speaker: SUMALEE
preceding_turns.text: Yes, I will do my best.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: I'll have an actual sketch for you next time.

preceding_turns.speaker: SUMALEE
preceding_turns.text: Thank you!

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: That's cool.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: We know that there are members of The Iron Shepherds here, and we know they have beds here. Wait until the fall of night?

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: Yeah.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: Take out the guards, kill them in their sleep?

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: I still would like some help, maybe one extra hand.

preceding_turns.speaker: ASHLEY
preceding_turns.text: How much time do we have until nightfall?
